Norah Pham

I'm an undergrad at UC Berkeley studying BioE & Business.

I like skateboarding, playing piano & flute, and embroidering.

Broadly, I'm interested in genetics (CRISPR), LGBTQ+/voter education policy, and strategy consulting, but am always exploring new things!

On campus, I'm part of Phoenix Consulting Group, Undergraduate Finance & Management Group, the Innovative Genomics Institute, and Kindness Club

Research

The Science Stuff

UC Berkeley · Innovative Genomics Institute

Advanced Translational Genetics Laboratory

I work with Dr. Hanqin Li and Dr. Isabela Ichihara de Barros at the ATG Lab within IGI.

I'm building a bacterial plasmid library to support large-scale CRISPR screens, and finding ways to make prime editing work more efficiently in stem cells.

R ImageJ PCR Bacterial Cloning Tissue Culturing Excel
UC Riverside · Riccomagno Neurodevelopmental Lab

AAV-Mediated Gene Delivery to the Brain

Tested different promoters with adeno-associated viruses (AAV) to deliver genetic cargo to the brain, discovering that a bioinformatic approach to identifying promoters can drive reporter expression in specific neural cell types.

Also conducted a project testing a transgenic mouse line to study axonal pruning, yielding insights into the molecular mechanisms underlying neurodevelopment.

Tldr: Figuring out how to precisely target the right cells in the brain using engineered viruses.

research presentation capstone paper
IHC PCR ImageJ Excel Tissue Culture Perfusions

Projects

Building Things Outside School/Lab

Python · Market Analysis

Cancer Market Sizing Tool

Built a Python-based market sizing tool that models the addressable patient population for cancer diagnostics across geographies and cancer types. The tool lets assumptions shift dynamically, swapping in a different region or indication, with the numbers updating accordingly.

Incorporated national statistics across multiple cancer indications to size both mail-in and lab-based testing markets. The analysis revealed a combined addressable market of ~109 million people in the US alone, with meaningful variation by risk category and testing modality.

Tldr: Turned a spreadsheet problem into a flexible, reproducible tool for market sizing in oncology diagnostics.

Python Pandas Oncology Market Sizing

output: market size breakdown by perceived risk & threshold: 109.31M total addressable population

Private Equity · Strategy

M.E.T. x Alpine Investors — Industry Disruption Analysis

Presented strategic recommendations to Alpine Investors, a $19B AUM mid-market private equity firm, in New York as part of UC Berkeley's M.E.T. Topics in Entrepreneurship course. My team and I analyzed two key industry disruptors: rising interest rates compressing buyout activity, and AI eroding traditional SaaS moats across Alpine's portfolio.

Delivered three actionable recommendations: a portfolio-wide AI exposure assessment mapped against a vulnerability matrix, a strategic pivot toward highly regulated sectors (security, compliance, healthcare) where regulatory complexity creates durable moats, and a shift from majority buyouts toward minority stakes to maintain deal flow without over-leveraging in a high-rate environment.

Tldr: Flew to New York to pitch a $19B PE firm on how AI and interest rates are reshaping private equity and built a live AI Exposure Analysis Tool to back it up.
